artPrompt: The tense moment of a rigged recount: a clawed goblin hand slamming a small token of folded paper onto a worn felt card table beside scattered coins and dice, a shadowed boss leaning in to demand the count be done again. A single low amber lamp burns over the table, cigar smoke hanging in the beam, the rest of the room drowned in noir black. Rendered as a moody oil painting — old-master chiaroscuro, visible impasto brushwork, deep shadow, warm varnished tones.
name: Buy the Recount
text:
Demand a recount of the last vote, coin flip, or die roll this turn — yes, you can recount a coin flip, this is Whateverville. Sacrifice a Favor token. You may set that result to whatever you wanted it to be.
If there has been no vote, flip, or roll this turn, recount the votes from an election that happened years ago. The Don wins that one too, retroactively.
flavorText: "Count them again." "We did, boss, three times." "Count them until they're right."
